title: CARLA: Adjusted common average referencing for cortico-cortical evoked potential data

funding_references.funder_name: R01 MH122258 CRCNS: Processing speed in the human connectome across the lifespan
T32 GM145408: Medical Scientist Training Program at Mayo Clinic

funding_references.award_title: CRCNS: Processing speed in the human connectome across the lifespan
Medical Scientist Training Program at Mayo Clinic

description: This dataset contains intracranial EEG recordings from four patients undergoing single pulse electrical stimulation at the Mayo Clinic. The data support the development and validation of CARLA (Adjusted Common Average Referencing), a method for improving signal quality in cortico-cortical evoked potential recordings. The dataset enables reproducible analysis of neural responses to standardized stimulation protocols and facilitates investigation of processing speed across the human connectome. Note: Data for figures 7 and 8 are unavailable and have been censored with 0 values in this release.
methods_description: Intracranial EEG recordings were collected from four patients at rest during single pulse electrical stimulation delivered at approximately 0.2 Hz. Stimulation parameters: 200 microsecond duration, biphasic waveform, 6 mA amplitude. Data are formatted according to BIDS version 1.14.0. Analysis code is available at https://github.com/hharveygit/CARLA_JNM.
